author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VIII. 26, 27.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The words of our Lord Jesus Christ, which He had addressed to the Jews, so regulating His discourse that the blind saw not, and believers’ eyes were opened, are these, which have been read to-day from the holy Gospel: “Then said the Jews, Who art thou?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because the Lord had said before, “If ye believe not that I am, ye shall die in your sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To this accordingly they rejoined, “Who art thou?” as if seeking to know on whom they ought to believe, so as not to die in their sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He replied to those who asked Him: “Who art thou?” by saying, “The beginning, for [so] also I speak to you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the Lord has called Himself the beginning, it may be inquired whether the Father also is the beginning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the Son who has a Father is the beginning, how much more easily must God the Father be understood as the beginning, who has indeed the Son whose Father He is, but has no one from whom He Himself proceedeth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Son is the Son of the Father, and the Father certainly is the Father of the Son; but the Son is called God of God,—the Son is called Light of Light; the Father is called Light, but not, of Light,—the Father is called God, but not, of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, God of God, Light of Light, is the beginning, how much more easily may we understand as such that Light, from whom the Light [cometh], and God, of whom is God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It seems, therefore, absurd, dearly beloved, to call the Son the beginning, and not to call the Father the beginning also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what shall we do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are there, then, two beginnings?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us beware of saying so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then, if both the Father is the beginning and the Son the beginning, how are there not two beginnings?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the same way that we call the Father God, and the Son God, and yet say not that there are two Gods; and yet He who is the Father is not the Son, He who is the Son is not the Father; and the Holy Spirit, the Spirit of the Father and of the Son, is neither the Father nor the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Although, then, as Catholic ears have been taught in the bosom of mother Church, neither He who is the Father is the Son, nor He who is the Son is the Father, nor is the Holy Spirit, of the Father and of the Son, either the Son or the Father, yet we say not that there are three Gods; although, if we are asked of each apart, we must, of whichever we are questioned, confess that He is God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But all this seems absurd to those who drag up familiar things to a level with things little known, visible things with invisible, and compare the creature to the Creator.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For unbelievers sometimes question us and say: Whom you call the Father, do you call him God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We answer, God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whom you call the Son, do you call him God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We answer, God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whom you call the Holy Spirit, do you call him God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We answer, God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, say they, are the Father, and the Son, and the Holy Spirit three Gods?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We answer, No. They are confounded, because they are not enlightened; they have their heart shut up, because they want the key of faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us then, brethren, by an antecedent faith that heals the eye of our heart, receive without obscurity what we understand,—and what we understand not, believe without hesitation; let us not quit the foundation of faith in order to reach the summit of perfection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father is God, the Son is God, the Holy Spirit is God: and yet He is not the Father who is the Son, nor He the Son who is the Father, and the Holy Spirit, the Spirit of the Father and the Son, is neither the Father nor the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Trinity is one God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Trinity is one eternity, one power, one majesty;—three, [but not three] Gods.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let not the reviler answer me: “Three what, then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For,” he adds, “if there are three, you must say, three what?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I reply: The Father, and the Son, and the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “See,” he says, “you have named three; but express what the three are?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nay, count them yourself; for I make out three when I say, the Father, and the Son, and the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Father is God as respects Himself, but [He is] the Father as respects the Son; the Son is God as respects Himself, but He is the Son as regards the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What I say you may gather from daily analogies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So it is with one man and another, if the one be a father, the other his son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is man as regards himself, but a father as regards his son; and the son man as respects himself, but a son as respects his father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For father is a name given relatively, and so with son; but these are two men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And certainly God the Father is Father in a relative sense, that is, in relation to the Son; and God the Son is Son relatively, that is, in relation to the Father; but not as the former are two men are these two Gods.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why is it not so here?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because that belongs to one sphere and this to another; for this is divine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is here something ineffable which cannot be explained in words, that there should both be, and not be, number.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For see if there appear not a kind of number, Father, and Son, and Holy Ghost—the Trinity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If three, three what?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here number fails.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so God neither keeps apart from number, nor is comprehended by number.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because there are three, there is a kind of number.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If you ask three what, number ceases.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence it is said, “Great is our Lord, and great His power; and of His understanding there is no number.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When you have begun to reflect, you begin to number; when you have numbered, you cannot tell what you have numbered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father is Father, the Son is Son, the Holy Spirit is the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What are these three, the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are They not three Gods?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No. Are They not three Almighties?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No. Not three Creators of the world?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No. Is the Father then almighty?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Manifestly almighty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And is the Son then not almighty?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Clearly the Son is also almighty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And is the Holy Spirit then not almighty?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, too, is almighty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are there then three Almighties?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No; only one Almighty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Only in Their relation to each other do They suggest number, not in Their essential existence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For though God the Father is, as respects Himself, God along with the Son and the Holy Spirit, there are not three Gods; and, though as respects Himself He is omnipotent, as well as the Son and the Holy Spirit, there are not three omnipotents; for in truth He is the Father not in respect to Himself, but to the Son; nor is the Son so in respect to Himself, but to the Father; nor is the Spirit so as regards Himself, in as far as He is called the Spirit of the Father and of the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have no name to give the three, save the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit, one God, one Almighty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so one beginning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Take an illustration from the Holy Scriptures, whereby you may in some measure comprehend what I am saying.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After our Lord Jesus Christ rose again, and was pleased to ascend into heaven, at the end of ten days He sent from thence the Holy Spirit, by whom those who were present in that one chamber were filled, and began to speak in the languages of all nations.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord’s murderers, terrified by the miracle, were pricked to the heart and sorrowed; sorrowing, were changed; and being changed, believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There were added to the Lord’s body, that is, to the number of believers, three thousand people.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so also by the working of another miracle there were added other five thousand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A considerable community was created, in which all, receiving the Holy Spirit, by whom spiritual love was kindled, were by their very love and fervor of spirit welded into one, and began in the very unity of fellowship to sell all that they had, and to lay the price at the apostles’ feet, that distribution might be made to every one as each had need.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Scripture says this of them, that “they were of one soul and one heart toward God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed then, brethren, and from this acknowledge the mystery of the Trinity, how it is we say, There is both the Father, and the Son, and the Holy Spirit, and yet there is one God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See! there were so many thousands of these, and yet there was one heart; there were so many thousands, and one soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How much more so God Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do I err at all in word when I call two men two souls, or three men three souls, or many men many souls?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely I speak correctly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them approach God, and one soul belongs to all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If by approaching God many souls by love become one soul, and many hearts one heart, what of the very fountain of love in the Father and Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it not still more so here that the Trinity is one God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thence, of that Holy Spirit, does love come to us, as the apostle says: “The love of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost, which is given unto us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If then the love of God, shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost which is given unto us, makes many souls one soul, and many hearts one heart, how much rather are the Father and Son and Holy Spirit, one God, one light, and one beginning?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us hear, then, the Beginning who speaks to us: “I have,” said He, “many things to say of you and to judge.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You remember that He said, “I do not judge any one.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, now He says, “I have many things to say of you and to judge.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, “I do not judge” is one thing: “I have to judge” is another; for He had come to save the world, not to judge the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In saying, “I have many things to say of you and to judge,” He speaks of the future judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For therefore did He ascend, that He may come to judge the living and the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No one will judge more justly than He who was unjustly judged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Many things,” said He, “have I to say of you and to judge; but He that sent me is true.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how the Son, His equal, gives glory to the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He sets us an example, and says as it were in our hearts: O believer, if thou hearest my gospel, the Lord thy God saith to thee, when I, in the beginning God the Word with God, equal with the Father, coeternal with Him that begat, give glory to Him whose Son I am, how canst thou be proud before Him, whose servant thou art?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I have many things,” He said, “to say of you and to judge: but He that sent me is true;” as if He had said, Therefore I judge the truth, because, as the Son of the True One, I am the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father true, the Son the truth,—which do we account the greater?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us reflect, if we can, which is the greater, the True One or the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Take some other instances.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is a pious man, or piety, the more comprehensive?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely piety itself; for the pious is derived from piety, not piety from the pious.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For piety may still exist, though he who was pious became impious.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has lost his piety, but has taken nothing from piety itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What also of comely and comeliness?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Comeliness is more than comely; for comeliness gives existence to the comely, not the comely to comeliness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so of chaste and chastity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Chastity is clearly something more than chaste.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if chastity had no existence, one would have no ground to be chaste; but though one may refuse to be chaste, chastity remains entire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If then the term piety implies more than the term pious, comeliness more than comely, chastity than chaste, shall we say that the Truth is more than the True One?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we say so, we shall begin to say that the Son is greater than the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord Himself says most distinctly, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, if the Son is the truth, what is the Father but what the Truth Himself says, “He that sent me is true”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son is the truth, the Father true.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I inquire which is the greater, but find equality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the true Father is true not because He contained a part of that truth, but because He begat it entire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I see I must speak more plainly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, not to detain you long, let me treat only of this point to-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When I have finished what, with God’s help, I wish to say, my discourse shall close.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have said this, then, to enlist your attention.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Every soul, as being a thing, is mutable; and although a great creature, yet a creature; though superior to the body, yet made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Every soul, then, since it is changeable—that is, sometimes believes, sometimes disbelieves; at one time wishes, at another time refuses; at one time is adulterous, at another chaste; now good, and again wicked,—is changeable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But God is that which is, and so has retained as His own peculiar name, “I am who am.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such also is the Son, when He says, “If ye believe not that I am;” and thereto pertains also, “Who art thou?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Beginning” (ver.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God therefore is unchangeable, the soul changeable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the soul receives from God the elements of its goodness it becomes good by participation, just as by participation thine eye seeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it sees not when the light is withdrawn, while so long as it shares in the light it sees.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since then by participation the soul is made good, if it changes and becomes bad, the goodness remains that made it good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there is a goodness of which it partook when good; and when it has turned to evil, that goodness continues entire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the soul fall away and become evil, there is no lessening of goodness; if it return and become good, that goodness is not enlarged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thine eye participates in this light, and thou seest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then thou hast not diminished the light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast not increased the light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By this illustration, brethren, understand that if the soul is pious, there is piety with God, of which the soul is partaker; if the soul is chaste, there is chastity with God, of which it partakes; if it is good, there is goodness with God, of which it partakes; if it is true, there is truth with God, of which the soul is partaker.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whereof if the soul is no partaker, every man is false; and if every man may be false, no man is true of himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the true Father is true of Himself, for He begat the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is one thing to say, That man is true, for he has taken in the truth: it is another, God is true, for He begat the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See then how God is true,—not by participating in, but by generating the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I see you have understood me, and am glad.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let this suffice you to-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The rest, according as He gives it, we shall expound when the Lord pleases.
